name: The Golden Girls
description: The 1985-1992 NBC sitcom — Dorothy, Rose, Blanche, and Sophia sharing a house in Miami and a lot of cheesecake.

questions.q: In which city do the Golden Girls share a house?

source.notes: The house was actually on a Disney studio backlot.

questions.q: Which dessert is the girls' go-to late-night kitchen ritual?

source.notes: Estimated over 100 cheesecakes consumed across the series.

questions.q: Which Emmy winner plays sharp-tongued Dorothy Zbornak?

source.notes: Arthur had previously starred in Maude.

questions.q: Which actress plays naïve Rose Nylund?

source.notes: White had previously played Sue Ann on Mary Tyler Moore.

questions.q: Which Southern-belle landlord of the house is famously man-hungry?

source.notes: Played by Rue McClanahan.

questions.q: Who is Dorothy's elderly Sicilian mother, living with the girls?

source.notes: Played by Estelle Getty, who was actually younger than Bea Arthur.

questions.q: On which U.S. network did The Golden Girls originally air?
What fictional town does Rose constantly reminisce about from her childhood?

source.notes: Rose's 'St. Olaf stories' were a running bit.

questions.q: Which U.S. state does Blanche Devereaux hail from?

source.notes: From Atlanta, Georgia; 'Big Daddy' is her father.

questions.q: What retirement home does Sophia escape at the start of the series?

source.notes: Running gag: 'Shady Pines, Ma!'

questions.q: Which two words traditionally kick off Sophia's Sicilian stories?

source.notes: 'Picture it: Sicily, 1922…'

questions.q: Which Golden Girl's husband died before the series begins, making her a widow?

source.notes: Blanche's George and Rose's Charlie both died pre-series.

questions.q: In the finale, which character gets remarried and moves away?

source.notes: Dorothy marries Blanche's uncle Lucas.

questions.q: The Golden Girls spun off a show in 1992-1993 that continued three of the leads. What was it called?

source.notes: McClanahan, White, and Getty — minus Bea Arthur's Dorothy.

questions.q: Who was Dorothy's ex-husband whose occasional reappearances drive plots?

source.notes: 'Yo-yo Stan,' played by Herb Edelman.

questions.q: What job did Dorothy hold for most of the series?

source.notes: Substitute teacher — Dorothy's clever asides.

questions.q: What was Rose's profession for most of the series?

source.notes: Worked at a local Miami grief counseling center.

questions.q: Which character boasts about her farmhouse Christmas memories from St. Olaf?

source.notes: Rose's relentlessly whimsical St. Olaf stories.

questions.q: Who originated the theme song 'Thank You for Being a Friend' — a #25 hit before the show used it?

source.notes: Andrew Gold released it in 1978; Cynthia Fee recorded the cover used as the theme.

questions.q: The Golden Girls won the Emmy for Outstanding Comedy Series how many times?

source.notes: Won in 1986 and 1987.

questions.q: Which actress is the only Golden Girl to win the Emmy for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series more than once?

source.notes: All four won Lead Actress (or Supporting) exactly once — a rare feat.

questions.q: Which Golden Girl passed away most recently, outliving the other three?

source.notes: Betty White died December 31, 2021, just shy of her 100th birthday.
